channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: The Florida attorney general's office is investigating Andrew and Tristan Tate
item.description: TALLAHASSEE, Fla. (AP) — Florida's attorney general announced Tuesday that his office has opened a criminal investigation Andrew and Tristan Tate, who are charged with human trafficking in Romania, days after they returned to the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Powerful US storms bring threats ranging from critical fire weather to blizzard conditions
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — Powerful storms tore the roofs off an apartment building and a nursing home in a small town in Oklahoma, damaged a high school near Dallas, and threatened more communities across the nation Tuesday with wide-ranging weather.
item.dc:creator: JEFF MARTIN and JACK BROOK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: IRS drafting plans to cut as much as half of its 90,000-person workforce, AP sources say
item.description: The IRS is drafting plans to cut its workforce by as much as half through a mix of layoffs, attrition and incentivized buyouts, according to two people familiar with the situation.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: FBI and DOJ headquarters are among more than 440 federal buildings listed for potential sale
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The Trump administration on Tuesday published a list of more than 400 federal properties it says it could close or sell, including the FBI headquarters and the main Department of Justice building, after deeming them “not core to government operations.
item.dc:creator: JILL COLVIN and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Two Oscar-winning films shed light on the thousands of disappeared people in Latin America
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — If there is a still open wound in Latin America, it is that of the tens of thousands of disappeared people and decades-long pain that has accumulated in parts of the region such as Mexico and Colombia.
item.dc:creator: FABIOLA SANCHEZ, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Arab leaders endorse Egypt's plan to rebuild Gaza as an alternative to Trump's proposal
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Arab leaders on Tuesday endorsed Egypt's postwar plan for the Gaza Strip that would allow its roughly 2 million Palestinians to remain, in a counterproposal to U.S. President Donald Trump’s plan to depopulate the territory and redevelop it as a beach destination.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY and MOHAMMAD JAHJOUH, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump wants to use the 'God Squad' to increase logging, but it must follow strict rules
item.description: President Donald Trump wants to increase logging in national forests and on public lands, including by bypassing endangered species protections.
item.dc:creator: TAMMY WEBBER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Man sentenced to 4 years in prison for Culver City hate crime assault
item.description: A previously homeless man has been sentenced to more than four years in prison for an unprovoked racially motivated assault on an Asian American woman in Culver City. Jesse Allen Lindsey, 38, pleaded guilty in December to a federal hate crime charge for the 2021 attack. Around 1 a.m.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KTLA 5

item.title: Idaho murders case: Bryan Kohberger judge ends 'pervasive' secret filings restricting what public can see
item.description: Judge overseeing Bryan Kohberger's Idaho student murders case says practice of filing many documents under seal violates the First Amendment and will end.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ukraine selling T-shirts with Zelensky quote from Trump-Vance meeting
item.description: The Ukrainian government has launched a T-shirt fundraising effort seeking to capitalize on Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky's now-infamous Oval Office meeting on Friday with President Trump and Vice President Vance.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump threatens increased tariffs if Canada retaliates
item.description: President Donald Trump on Tuesday threatened to add additional taxes on Canada if the U.S.'s northern neighbor Canada retaliates with its own after Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau slammed Trump’s newly implemented tariffs.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump's speech to Congress will offer divided nation an accounting of turbulent first weeks
item.description: President Donald Trump will stand before a joint session of Congress on Tuesday to give an accounting of his turbulent first weeks in office as a divided nation struggles to keep pace, with some Americans fearing for the country's future while others are cheering him on.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Jimmy Johnson retires from Fox Sports after being face of NFL coverage for most of 3 decades
item.description: Jimmy Johnson, the Pro Football Hall of Fame coach who won two Super Bowls and a college national championship, announced his retirement from Fox Sports on Monday after being part of its NFL coverage for most of the network's 31 years.
item.dc:creator: Eric Olson, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Retired US military leaders support letting Israel 'finish the job' against Iran, proxies
item.description: Dozens of retired U.S. military leaders urge President Donald Trump to throw America's support behind Israel in its fight against a nuclear Iran.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: IHOP offers free short stack for National Pancake Day
item.description: Calling all pancake lovers! If you are ready to satisfy your craving, IHOP is celebrating National Pancake Day on March 4 with a free treat.
item.dc:creator: Odyssey Fields, WFLA, NBC

item.title: State forgives more than $7M in medical debt through new initiative
item.description: Medical debt forgiven through the initiative is considered a no-strings-attached gift that does not count as earned income or incur taxes.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Bawden, NewsNation

item.title: More endangered Mexican gray wolves are roaming the southwestern US, annual survey shows
item.description: ALBUQUERQUE, N.M. (AP) — It is a ritual that takes place every winter — federal and state wildlife managers use remote cameras, scat collection, radio telemetry devices and helicopters to count Mexican gray wolves that are roaming mountain ranges in parts of New Mexico and Arizona.
item.dc:creator: SUSAN MONTOYA BRYAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Fargo has an election method that helps mainstream candidates. North Dakota lawmakers may ban it
item.description: BISMARCK, N.D. (AP) — When Fargo voters approved a unique local election system in 2018, their goal was to choose candidates with broader support to lead North Dakota’s largest city. It worked — maybe too well.
item.dc:creator: JACK DURA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Las Vegas to host World Sign Spinning Championship
item.description: “It’s really neat because on an average day, when we’re out spinning for a business, we’ll spin for close to 20,000 people, that’s about how many cars drive by in a day.
item.dc:creator: JC Fernandez, NewsNation

item.title: Hateful message left on Ohio restaurant receipt results in outpouring of support, staff says
item.description: The public is rallying behind a central Ohio Mexican restaurant after a customer left a hateful message instead of a tip.
item.dc:creator: Anna Hoffman, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Wall Street falls again as losses wipe out all post-election gains for the S&P 500
item.description: Stocks tumbled on Wall Street Tuesday as a trade war between the U.S. and its key trading partners escalated and threatened to wipe out all the gains for the S&P 500 since Election Day.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Pope stable, but will sleep with ventilation mask, Vatican says
item.description: Pope Francis was in stable condition Tuesday and breathing with just the help of supplemental oxygen after respiratory crises a day earlier, but will resume using a ventilation mask at night, the Vatican said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: How South Carolina's execution of a condemned killer by firing squad will unfold
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — When a South Carolina man who killed his ex-girlfriend’s parents with a baseball bat steps into the death row chamber Friday night, it won’t be lethal injection or electrocution that ends his life.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: AG Pam Bondi says FBI delivered 'truckload' of Epstein files after she put out hard deadline
item.description: AG Pam Bondi said the FBI handed over a "truckload" of Epstein files after she gave the agency until 8 a.m. Friday morning to turn them over. She also praised FBI boss Kash Patel.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Man pleads guilty to murder and attempted murder in shooting at suburban Chicago July 4 parade
item.description: WAUKEGAN, Ill. (AP) — An Illinois man pleaded guilty Monday to killing seven people and injuring dozens more when he opened fire on a 2022 Independence Day parade in a Chicago suburb, a stunning development moments before opening statements in his trial on murder and attempted murder charges.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIA TAREEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: GOP rep introduces measure putting Trump face on $100 bill
item.description: First-term GOP Rep. Brandon Gill (Texas) has introduced a measure that would require all $100 bills to have President Trump’s face on them.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KTLA 5

item.title: U.S. stocks plummet after Trump announces tariffs on Canada and Mexico
item.description: U.S. stocks fell sharply Monday after President Trump announced tariffs on Canada and Mexico, while the Dow Jones Industrial Average was down 735 points, or 1.7%, with 45 minutes remaining in trading.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: New York City reports measles cases, raising public health concern
item.description: Measles is a highly contagious virus that can infect up to 90% of unvaccinated people who come in close proximity to an infected person, according to the New York City Department of Health.
item.dc:creator: Ben Mitchell, NewsNation

item.title: US stocks tumble after Trump announces Canadian and Mexican tariffs
item.description: U.S. stocks are falling sharply Monday after President Donald Trump said tariffs he announced on Canada and Mexico will take effect on Tuesday. The bond market also took a hit following the latest warning signal on the strength of the U.S. economy.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Pope Francis suffers new breathing crises, is back on noninvasive ventilation: Vatican
item.description: Pope Francis suffered two new episodes of acute respiratory crises Monday and was put back on noninvasive mechanical ventilation, the Vatican said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Crews battle wildfires in North and South Carolina amid dry conditions and gusty winds
item.description: Crews battled wildfires in North and South Carolina on Sunday amid dry conditions and gusty winds as residents were forced to evacuate in some areas.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: The Latest: The 97th Academy Awards are underway
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — The 97th Academy Awards have kicked off at the Dolby Theatre in Los Angeles. “Emilia Pérez” leads the pack with 13 Oscar nominations, but “Wicked” and “The Brutalist” are close behind. The 2025 Oscars opened with its biggest musical voices.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Topsy-turvy Oscar season concludes today: Here’s what to look for at the show
item.description: Sunday’s Academy Awards will bring to a close one of the most unpredictable Oscar races in recent memory.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Tennessee could add 'covenant marriage' with proposed bill
item.description: One Tennessee lawmaker would like to add a new kind of marriage to the books through a bill filed in the General Assembly.
item.dc:creator: Erin McCullough, ABC 4

item.title: Lifelong New York civil rights advocate and NAACP leader Hazel Dukes dies at 92
item.description: Hazel Dukes, the president of the New York State chapter of the NAACP and lifelong civil rights advocate, died Saturday at the age of 92.
item.dc:creator: SEJAL GOVINDARAO, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Utah senators react to contentious Oval Office meeting between Trump and Zelenskyy
item.description: Utah's senators are responding to a contentious interaction between Trump and Vance and Zelenskyy Friday with contrasting public statements.
item.dc:creator: Aubree B. Jennings, ABC 4
